note: Mathlib/Order/SetNotation.lean:44 -- class SupSet (α : Type*) where sSup : Set α → α, the supremum operation (join of a set) in a preorder; the general formalization of 'supremum' underlying ConditionallyCompleteLattice/CompleteLattice.

Mathlib/Order/SetNotation.lean:50 -- class InfSet (α : Type*) where sInf : Set α → α, the infimum operation (meet of a set) in a preorder; the general formalization of 'infimum', paired with SupSet for the same QID.

Mathlib/Computability/DFA.lean:58 -- structure DFA (α σ) with step/start/accept fields; docstring 'A Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA) is a state machine which decides membership in a particular Language'. Formalizes the deterministic variant of a finite-state machine, not the fully general notion (no output/Mealy-Moore machines), so 'related' rather than 'exact'.

Mathlib/Order/Heyting/Basic.lean:155 -- class HeytingAlgebra; file docstring explicitly states 'Heyting algebras precisely model intuitionistic logic, whereas Boolean algebras model classical logic.' This is the algebraic-semantics model, not a formalized proof calculus for intuitionistic logic itself, hence 'related'.

Mathlib/Data/Nat/Choose/Basic.lean:63 -- theorem choose_succ_succ (n k) : choose (succ n) (succ k) = choose n k + choose n (succ k); this is Pascal's rule, the defining recurrence of Pascal's triangle (Nat.choose values are literally the triangle's entries; see also 'sum of entries in a row of Pascal's triangle' docstrings in Choose/Sum.lean).

verified via decl-name oracle (docLink ./Init/Core.html#Std.Associative, kind class) -- core Lean/Std class marking a binary operation op as associative (op (op a b) c = op a (op b c)); Mathlib's own mul_assoc/add_assoc (Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Defs.html#mul_assoc) instantiate it per-structure. This class IS the abstract formalization of the associative property.

Verified 'Projectivization' is a key under libraries.Mathlib.modules.LinearAlgebra.sub in catalog/data/hierarchy.json. Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Projectivization/Basic.lean:14 -- 'This file contains the definition of the projectivization of a vector space over a field, as well as the bijection between said projectivization and the collection of all one dimensional subspaces', plus Subspace.lean/Collinear.lean/Independence.lean/Action.lean/PSL/* build out points, lines, collinearity and projective transformations -- this folder is projective geometry's formal home in Mathlib.
